response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced Staff/Senior Software Engineer with over 30 years of professional experience — a dramatic mismatch for an entry-level junior position. While he has the technical foundations (CS degree, Java listed in skills, agile practices, cloud experience), two critical disqualifiers stand out: his German is self-described as only 'Basic,' directly contradicting the role's requirement for strong verbal and written German communication in a client-facing consulting context; and his seniority level makes him fundamentally unsuited for a junior IC role, creating retention and role-fit risks regardless of technical capability.
strengths: CS degree and broad technical background covering Java, OOP principles, and cloud technologies (AWS CDK)
Deep agile and software craftsmanship experience including TDD, CI/CD, pair programming, and trunk-based development
Demonstrated ability to work in client-facing and collaborative team environments across many projects
concerns: German proficiency is listed as 'Basic' — the role explicitly requires strong verbal and written German for client collaboration in Germany
30+ years of senior/staff-level experience makes this candidate severely overqualified for a junior IC position
Primary technology stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not Java; Java appears only as a peripheral skill with limited recent usage
